info.title: ChatCLI AIOps Platform API
info.description:
REST API for the ChatCLI AIOps Platform — incidents, SLOs, runbooks, approvals, postmortems, analytics, audit, federation and health.

The playground below uses **editable server variables** so you can point requests at your own self-hosted instance. Adjust `host`, `port` and `basePath` to match your deployment, then click **Try it**.

servers.description: Self-hosted ChatCLI AIOps Platform — adjust host, port and basePath to match your deployment.

host.description: Hostname or IP of your ChatCLI operator. For Kubernetes, use the Service DNS (e.g. chatcli-api.chatcli-system.svc) or your Ingress host.

port.description: Port the API listens on. Default is 8090; configurable via Helm value `apiPort` or env var CHATCLI_API_PORT.

basePath.description: API base path. Always `api/v1` unless you have a custom proxy rewrite in front of the operator.

tags.name: Incidents
tags.description: Incident lifecycle: list, fetch, acknowledge, resolve, snooze, timeline and remediation views.

tags.name: SLOs
tags.description: Service Level Objectives and error budgets.

tags.name: Postmortems
tags.description: Auto-generated postmortems with review, feedback and close.

tags.name: Remediations
tags.description: Remediation plans and agentic execution history.

tags.name: AI Insights
tags.description: AI root-cause analysis and recommendations per incident.

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: Operational analytics: MTTD, MTTR, trends, capacity, compliance.

tags.name: Federation
tags.description: Multi-cluster federation: clusters, status and cross-cluster correlations.

get.description: Returns a list of incidents with optional filters and pagination.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp — only return incidents detected at or after this time.

get.description: Returns full details of a specific incident.
Returns the remediation plan and execution status for a given incident.
Returns the full event timeline for an incident.

post.description: Marks an incident as acknowledged, signalling that the team is aware and acting.

acknowledgedBy.description: User or team identifier acknowledging the incident.

post.description: Manually resolves an incident — used when the system escalates to human action or an operator confirms the issue is fixed.

resolution.description: Description of how the incident was resolved. Defaults to `"Manually resolved via dashboard"` when omitted.

example.resolution: Memory leak fixed in payment-service v2.4.1 hotfix deployed manually. Verified stable for 15 minutes.

post.description: Snoozes notifications for an incident for a specified duration.

duration.description: Snooze duration in Go duration format (e.g. `30m`, `1h`, `2h30m`). Max 24h.

reason.example: Deploy in progress, will revisit after rollout completes.

example.reason: Deploy in progress, will revisit after rollout completes.

get.description: Returns the list of configured remediation runbooks.

parameters.name: automated
parameters.description: Filter by execution type: `true` = automated, `false` = manual.

example.name: runbook-disk-cleanup
example.description: Automatic disk cleanup when usage exceeds 85%.

steps.name: cleanup-temp-files
steps.description: Remove temp files older than 7 days

delete.description: Deletes a remediation runbook. Runbooks in use by active incidents cannot be deleted.

get.description: Returns the list of configured Service Level Objectives.
Returns full details of a specific SLO.
Returns the SLO error budget breakdown, including consumption history.
Returns the list of pending and historical approval requests.
Returns details of a specific approval request, including the pending action, blast-radius assessment and approval history.

post.description: Approves a pending request, allowing the remediation to proceed.

comment.example: Approved — AI analysis confirms memory bump is required.

example.comment: Approved — AI analysis confirms memory bump is required.

post.description: Rejects a pending approval request, blocking the remediation.

reason.example: Prefer adjusting HPA before raising limits.

example.reason: Prefer adjusting HPA before raising limits.

get.description: Returns the list of postmortems generated by the platform.

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: Filter by severity of the originating incident.

get.description: Returns the full postmortem for an incident, including root-cause analysis, timeline and action items.

post.description: Moves a postmortem into the `InReview` state, signalling that a human is reviewing the AI-generated analysis.

notes.example: Root cause confirmed. Adding additional action item for rate limiting.

example.notes: Root cause confirmed. Adding additional action item for rate limiting.

post.description: Submits human feedback on a postmortem — lets the developer rate remediation accuracy, override the root cause and add comments.

providedBy.description: Identifier of the feedback author. Max 253 chars.

overrideRootCause.description: Human-provided root cause overriding AI analysis. Max 4096 chars.

example.overrideRootCause: Leak caused by uncancelled goroutine in webhook handler, not the response body.
example.comments: Good analysis. Suggest AdjustResources before restart next time.

post.description:
Acknowledges that the human follow-up for a Contained incident has been performed (rollback, config fix, etc.). Sets the platform.chatcli.io/human-action-acknowledged annotation that the PostMortemReconciler watches; once set, the PostMortem becomes closeable.

Requires the parent Issue to have transitioned to Contained and the PostMortem to be marked with spec.requiresHumanAction=true. Added by GAP-03 fix (chaos test report 2026-05-23).

acknowledgedBy.description: Identifier of the person/team that performed the required action.

note.description: Free-text note describing the action taken.
note.example: Rolled back to v1.2.3 and scaled replicas back to 3.

example.note: Rolled back to v1.2.3 and scaled replicas back to 3.

200.description: Human action acknowledged. The PostMortem is now closeable.

400.description: PostMortem does not require human action — nothing to acknowledge.

post.description:
Closes a postmortem after review. Closed postmortems are immutable.

Note (GAP-03 fix): when the PostMortem has spec.requiresHumanAction=true, the PostMortemReconciler will REVERT premature closures back to Open until the aiops.chatcli.io/human-action-acknowledged annotation is set. Use POST /postmortems/{name}/ack-human-action before closing.

reviewer.description: Identifier of the person closing the postmortem.

notes.description: Final notes / summary of the review outcome.
notes.example: All action items assigned. Approved for compliance archive.

example.notes: All action items assigned. Root cause verified. Approved for compliance archive.

get.description: Returns all remediation plans with execution state, actions and agentic-mode details.
Returns full remediation plan details, including execution evidence, rollback info, safety constraints and agentic conversation history.
Returns all AI-generated insights with root-cause analysis, confidence scores and suggested remediation actions per incident.
Returns the full AI analysis for a specific insight, including all enrichment contexts and suggested actions.
Returns a consolidated summary of platform operational metrics.
Returns incident trends over time with severity breakdown.
Returns the resources with the highest incident counts.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of resources to return.

get.description: Returns Mean Time To Detect metrics — time between event occurrence and platform detection.
Returns detailed MTTR metrics with phase breakdown.
Returns resource capacity forecasts based on historical trends — identifies resources at risk of exhaustion.
Returns a compliance report with incident, remediation, SLA and approval metrics for the requested time window.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End date (RFC3339). Default: now. Default window: 7 days.

get.description: Returns remediation success rates grouped by primary strategy.
Returns the audit log with all platform actions.
Exports the full audit log in CSV or JSON for compliance and external analysis. Large exports return a `202 Accepted` with an async download URL.

202.description: Async export job for large result sets

get.description: Returns the list of Kubernetes clusters managed by the platform.
Returns consolidated status across all federated clusters.
Returns details of a specific federated cluster, including health status, node count, resource usage and active incidents.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Unique registration name of the cluster.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Kubernetes namespace where the ClusterRegistration CR resides.

get.description: Returns all federated clusters, optionally filtered by tier.
Returns cross-cluster incident correlations — incidents with the same root cause detected on multiple clusters.
Returns global status across all federated clusters.
Liveness/readiness probe for the AIOps operator. Returns `200 OK` if the operator process is running. No authentication required.

example.status: not ready: failed to list namespaces: connection refused

ApiKeyAuth.name: Authorization
ApiKeyAuth.description: Bearer token issued by the operator. Format: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded for this token's role

Conflict.description: Conflict with current state of the resource

code.description: Top-level code (only used by some endpoints).

state.description: Lifecycle state. Contained (GAP-03 fix) indicates the workload was silenced via a containment action and human intervention is required to restore service.

requiresHumanAction.description: True when the Issue is in Contained state OR carries the RequiresHumanAction condition. Surfaced by GAP-03 fix so dashboards can flag pending follow-ups without parsing conditions.

chaosInduced.description: True when the Issue was correlated with an active ChaosExperiment at creation time (label platform.chatcli.io/source=chaos-experiment). GAP-04 fix.

chaosExperiment.description: Name of the correlated ChaosExperiment CR when chaosInduced=true. GAP-04 fix.

requiresHumanAction.description: Marks PostMortems pending a human follow-up after a Contained incident. GAP-03 fix.

requiredAction.description: Concrete action a human needs to take to fully resolve the incident. GAP-03 fix.

chaosInduced.description: True for PostMortems generated from chaos drills. GAP-04 fix.

chaosExperiment.description: Name of the ChaosExperiment CR when chaosInduced=true. GAP-04 fix.

resourceMeta.description: Used by some endpoints in place of metadata.

requiresHumanAction.description: True when the parent Issue was only Contained (workload silenced, root cause not fixed). PostMortemReconciler will not allow Closed transition while this is true and the human-action-acknowledged annotation is absent. GAP-03 fix.

requiredAction.description: Concrete follow-up action a human must take (e.g., 'restore deployment replicas after fixing root cause'). Set together with requiresHumanAction. GAP-03 fix.

chaosInduced.description: True when this PostMortem belongs to a chaos drill (parent Issue carried platform.chatcli.io/source=chaos-experiment). GAP-04 fix.

chaosExperiment.description: Name of the ChaosExperiment CR correlated with the incident. GAP-04 fix.

summary.description: Aggregated counters returned at the top level of the spec body. As of the GAP-03/04 fixes, dashboards rely on containedIssues, postMortemsRequiringHumanAction and chaosInducedIssues to distinguish truly resolved incidents from contained ones and to keep chaos drills out of production trend widgets.

containedIssues.description: Issues in the Contained state (silenced via containment, awaiting human action). Counted as 'open' AND tracked here separately. GAP-03 fix.

chaosInducedIssues.description: Issues correlated with an active ChaosExperiment at creation time. Excluded from production MTTD/MTTR histograms. GAP-04 fix.

postMortemsRequiringHumanAction.description: PostMortems with spec.requiresHumanAction=true that are NOT yet Closed. Surfaced as a dedicated dashboard card to prevent incident closure metrics from masking unresolved bugs. GAP-03 fix.
